frameworks.name: peripheral-foveal integration
frameworks.one_line: Peripheral and foveal vision interact rather than processing independently across the visual field.

frameworks.name: transsaccadic integration
frameworks.one_line: Signals are stitched and averaged across saccades to reduce perceptual uncertainty.

frameworks.name: retinotopic cortical processing
frameworks.one_line: Foveal retinotopic areas host processing of peripheral signals for object recognition.

core_question: How do peripheral and foveal vision interact to yield a stable, continuous perceptual experience despite dramatic processing differences across the visual field?
key_assumptions: Visual processing differs systematically from retina to cortex across eccentricity
Perceptual experience is subjectively stable and continuous
Peripheral and foveal streams are not independent
manipulations: Comparison of peripheral vs foveal processing during fixation
key_findings: Peripheral signals are processed in foveal retinotopic areas to support peripheral object recognition
Foveal information is extrapolated toward the periphery to homogenize the representation
Transsaccadic feature changes reduce peripheral-foveal appearance discrepancy
Peripheral and foveal signals are integrated across saccades to average percepts and reduce uncertainty
interpretations: Peripheral and foveal processing are closely connected, balancing wide field coverage with foveal high resolution
Cross-eccentricity interactions support perceptual stability and continuity
